# fupit
> Public climate and habitability projection app for any location worldwide, built from grounded climate datasets.

## About
fupit is a free public web application that lets anyone search for a location on Earth and view future climate and habitability projections to 2100. The serving engine uses an offline CMIP6/IPCC grid, NASA/IPCC sea-level projections, ETCCDI extreme-climate indices, and a Natural Earth 1:110m nearest-coast screen for sea-level relevance wording. No account required.

Numbers must be traceable to source data and documented methods. If a value cannot be grounded, fupit should omit it rather than fabricate it.

Source repository: https://github.com/MikkoParkkola/climate
Builder profile: https://github.com/MikkoParkkola

## Priority Pages

/ : Single-location climate projection tool — search for a city or place and explore its projected future climate across multiple decades and emissions scenarios, with raw JSON and Markdown educational summary export.
/comparison : Multi-location climate comparison page — compare climate projections side-by-side for two or more locations to understand regional differences.
/rankings : Bounded top-10 ranking page — compares precomputed climate signals across documented curated-city, Natural Earth population-place, and Natural Earth-derived country aggregate catalogs with source receipts and caveats.
/methodology : Methodology and source page — explains CMIP6/IPCC data, IPCC calibration, sea-level inputs, Natural Earth coastal relevance caveats, ETCCDI extremes, risk thresholds, habitability scoring, and known limitations.
/data-quality : Build evidence page — reports current artifact hashes, source-registry rows, default-scenario policy source IDs, ranking catalog coverage, trajectory-audit coverage, validation-report status, trend-review flags, and known launch limitations.

## Audience
General public, researchers, journalists, educators, planners, and policymakers interested in how climate change may affect specific locations.
